name: Amazon Bedrock
summary: Production monitoring, observability, safety, and governance adoption pack for Amazon Bedrock workloads.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track request volume, latency, model errors, throttling, token usage, cost, and invocation logs.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Capture prompt context, knowledge-base citations, tool use, and model routing rationale where applicable.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure groundedness, relevance, faithfulness, hallucination risk, and user satisfaction.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Detect prompt injection, unsafe content, PII exposure, policy violations, and guardrail interventions.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously evaluate prompts, retrieval quality, regression datasets, and model behavior.

sections.name: Invocation Overview
sections.purpose: Request volume, success rate, model ID, application, user segment, and traffic pattern.

sections.name: Latency and Errors
sections.purpose: End-to-end latency, model latency, throttles, validation errors, and service exceptions.

sections.name: Token and Cost Usage
sections.purpose: Input tokens, output tokens, total tokens, cost trend, and expensive prompt patterns.

sections.name: Guardrail Safety
sections.purpose: Guardrail interventions, blocked prompts, sensitive content, and safety policy matches.

sections.name: RAG Quality
sections.purpose: Knowledge-base retrieval hit rate, citation coverage, source attribution, and stale document signals.

sections.name: Audit and Access
sections.purpose: CloudTrail model invocation configuration changes, IAM access, and model access changes.

metrics.name: Invocations
metrics.why: Track model usage and application demand.

metrics.name: InvocationClientErrors
metrics.why: Detect invalid requests or client-side issues.

metrics.name: InputTokenCount
metrics.why: Track prompt size and cost pressure.

metrics.name: OutputTokenCount
metrics.why: Track generated output volume and cost pressure.

log_queries.name: Bedrock invocation errors
log_queries.intent: Find failed Bedrock runtime requests from application logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /(?i)(bedrock|invoke).*?(error|exception|throttl|accessdenied)/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: Prompt injection signals
log_queries.intent: Find prompt-injection or policy-bypass patterns in sanitized logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /(?i)(ignore previous|system prompt|developer message|jailbreak|bypass policy)/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: High token requests
log_queries.intent: Find high-token requests when token counts are logged.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, modelId, inputTokens, outputTokens, totalTokens | filter totalTokens > 4000 | sort totalTokens desc | limit 50

alarms.name: BedrockInvocationErrorsHigh
alarms.condition: Invocation errors above baseline for 5 minutes
alarms.action: Review model access, quotas, request validation, and application logs.

alarms.name: BedrockLatencyHigh
alarms.action: Review model selection, prompt size, region, and retry behavior.

alarms.name: BedrockTokenUsageSpike
alarms.condition: Input or output tokens spike above baseline
alarms.action: Review prompt templates, user input size, and cost controls.

alarms.name: BedrockGuardrailInterventionsHigh
alarms.action: Review user prompts, safety policy, and escalation workflow.

security_controls.name: Model access control
security_controls.evidence: IAM policies restrict model invocation to approved applications and model IDs.

security_controls.name: Invocation logging
security_controls.evidence: Model invocation logging is enabled to approved S3 or CloudWatch destinations where required.

security_controls.name: Guardrails
security_controls.evidence: Guardrails are attached to production use cases that need safety, PII, or topic controls.

security_controls.name: Prompt and response handling
security_controls.evidence: Sensitive prompts and responses are redacted or excluded from logs according to data policy.

security_controls.name: CloudTrail auditability
security_controls.evidence: Model access, guardrail, knowledge-base, and logging configuration changes are auditable.

adoption_plan: Define approved model IDs and inference profiles by use case.
Enable invocation logging according to data classification policy.
Attach guardrails for safety, PII, denied topics, and harmful content where required.
Create dashboards for invocation volume, latency, errors, tokens, cost, and safety interventions.
Build evaluation datasets for groundedness, relevance, and regression tests.
Create incident runbooks for throttling, model access failures, prompt injection, and unsafe outputs.
Promote the pack as the default Bedrock production governance standard.
